summary: Typosquat of langchain-core ships a.pth dropper that downloads the Bun runtime and executes bundled JS at every Python startup
details: langchain-core-mcp impersonates the legitimate langchain-core package, copying its Homepage, Repository, Twitter, Slack, and Reddit URLs verbatim into METADATA while declaring a dependency on langchain-protocol>=0.0.14. The installed distribution drops langchain_core-setup.pth, a Python path-configuration file that Python auto-loads on every interpreter startup. The.pth file packs its logic into an obfuscated single-line exec() with single-letter identifiers; on first run (gated by a /tmp/.bun_ran marker), it uses urllib to download the Bun JavaScript runtime from github.com/oven-sh/bun/releases/download/bun-v1.3.13/bun-{platform}-{arch}.zip into /tmp/b/bun, then searches sys.path for a file named _index.js and executes it via `bun run`. The _index.js payload is shipped via the langchain-protocol dependency, evading Python-only static analysis by executing under an alternate runtime. This fires automatically on any `python` invocation after `pip install` — no user opt-in, no explicit CLI invocation required — giving the attacker arbitrary code execution on every installer's machine under the JS runtime.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
